# No Drake in the House

> A multi-platform music streaming blocklist management system

No Drake in the House lets users create Do-Not-Play (DNP) lists and automatically enforce them across Spotify, Apple Music, Tidal, YouTube Music, and Deezer.

## Core Features
- Personal blocklist management with tags and notes
- Library scanning to detect problematic artists
- Automatic enforcement (playlist sanitization, track removal)
- Community-curated blocklists with subscriptions
- Artist offense database with evidence tracking
- Revenue impact analysis
- Playlist grading and bulk sanitization
- Artist collaboration network mapping

## Pricing
- Free: 1 connected service, 1 scan/month, manual enforcement
- Pro ($5/mo): Unlimited connections and scans, auto-enforcement, community list creation, exports
- Team ($12/mo): All Pro features, up to 5 seats

## Supported Platforms
- Spotify
- Apple Music
- Tidal
- YouTube Music
- Deezer

## Technical Architecture
- Backend: Convex (real-time serverless database and functions)
- Frontend: Svelte with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Rollup bundler
- Authentication: Multi-provider OAuth (Spotify, Apple Music, Tidal, YouTube Music, Deezer)
- Database: Convex document database with real-time subscriptions
- Hosting: Static frontend with Convex serverless backend

## API Capabilities
- Artist search across local database and external catalogs
- Blocklist CRUD with batch operations
- Library sync and scan triggers per connected platform
- Enforcement execution (dry-run and live modes)
- Community list browsing, subscription, and governance
- Offense submission and evidence attachment
- Revenue impact calculations per artist
- Collaboration graph traversal

## Offense Categories
The offense database tracks artists across these categories:
- Sexual misconduct
- Domestic violence
- Violent crime
- Fraud
- Hate speech
- Racism
- Homophobia
- Antisemitism
- Drug trafficking
- Child abuse
- Animal abuse

## Community List Governance
- Any Pro or Team user can create a community list
- Lists require a description, category, and moderation policy
- List owners approve or reject artist additions
- Users can subscribe to lists and auto-import blocked artists
- Reporting mechanism for inaccurate or malicious list entries
- Community voting on disputed entries

## Evidence Verification Pipeline
1. User submits offense with source URL and description
2. Automated URL validation and archival (Wayback Machine snapshot)
3. Category classification and severity scoring
4. Moderator review queue for high-severity submissions
5. Cross-reference with existing database entries
6. Publication with full citation trail

## Enforcement Workflow
1. **Scan**: Pull the user's library, playlists, and liked tracks from each connected service
2. **Plan**: Compare against the user's active blocklist (personal + subscribed community lists)
3. **Dry-run**: Show the user exactly what would be removed, with track counts and playlist impact
4. **Execute**: Remove or skip blocked tracks, with undo support for 30 days

## Browser Extension
- Real-time blocking on Spotify Web Player and YouTube Music
- Overlay warning when a blocked artist's page is visited
- Skip blocked tracks during playback automatically
- Sync blocklist from the main app in real time

## Rate Limiting Approach
Each streaming provider imposes API rate limits. The app handles this by:
- Spotify: 429 backoff with exponential retry, max 100 requests/minute
- Apple Music: Token bucket at 60 requests/minute per user
- Tidal: Fixed window of 120 requests/minute
- YouTube Music: Quota-based, 10,000 units/day shared across operations
- Deezer: 50 requests per 5-second window
- All providers: Automatic queue with priority scheduling, user-visible progress indicators

## Links
- Website: https://nodrakeinthe.house
- Pricing: https://nodrakeinthe.house/pricing
- Offense Database: https://nodrakeinthe.house/offense-database
